qa_pairs.question: How can one retrieve a list of available GPU model specifiers, including Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) specifiers, on a cluster using Slurm, especially if the documentation is outdated?
qa_pairs.answer: You can obtain the available GPU model specifiers from Slurm using the command: `sinfo -o "%G"|grep gpu|sed 's/gpu://g'|sed 's/),/\n/g'|cut -d: -f1|sort|uniq`. However, this command will not provide short synonyms that may be available for some MIG specifiers at certain sites.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it crucial to explicitly provide a specific GPU model specifier in Slurm job scripts, rather than omitting it?
qa_pairs.answer: It is strongly recommended to always provide a specific GPU model specifier in your job scripts because if you do not, your job may be rejected or sent to an arbitrary GPU instance. Most programs cannot efficiently use an arbitrary GPU.

qa_pairs.question: When requesting GPU resources for a Slurm job, what are the default and recommended practices for allocating CPU cores and system memory?
qa_pairs.answer: Along with each GPU instance, a Slurm job defaults to one CPU core. For optimal performance, users should also request a specific amount of system memory. The recommended maximum numbers of CPU cores and gigabytes of system memory per GPU instance are detailed in the "table of bundle characteristics."
